title: 2 Questions about Kaworu
wikitext:
[]: 2 Questions about Kaworu             Quote Post by The Goose » Sat Dec 27, 2008 6:10 pm 1. Why didn't he kill himself, instead of asking Shinji to? 2. If he was against killing humanity, why did he choose to die? Why didn't he try to help take down Seele?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 27, 2008 8:43 pm 1) My guess is he wasn't able to kill himself if Angels are apparently immortal in some way. (Without the Eva around I guess?) Or maybe it's a SoL thing. 2) Good question. If the Eva2 game is any indication, Shinji can talk Kaworu out of dying and he becomes a regular Eva pilot, but I'm really not sure why in the anime this possibility wasn't mentioned. Other than the fact Kaworu's death symbolizes Shinji rejecting the ideal (Kaworu and later Instrumentality) for the real (Asuka and later reality).

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Dec 27, 2008 9:32 pm 1) Perhaps he isn't capable of killing himself? 2) Even if Shinji had let him live, Nerv probably wouldn't. With Kaworu around, the risks would be too great to ignore.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sat Dec 27, 2008 9:42 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: My guess is he wasn't able to kill himself if Angels are apparently immortal in some way. (Without the Eva around I guess?) Or maybe it's a SoL thing.

[]: Re: 2 Questions about Kaworu             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 27, 2008 11:52 pm The Goose wrote: 1. Why didn't he kill himself, instead of asking Shinji to?

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Dec 28, 2008 12:31 am He says "I'm destined to live forever", for whatever that's worth.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 28, 2008 2:04 am Where the heck would they "mention" it?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Dec 28, 2008 2:24 am Now Adam saying "Just kidding, lets fight!" might achieve stranger results... Reichu wrote: He says "I'm destined to live forever", for whatever that's worth.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 28, 2008 10:07 am it is IMPOSSIBLE for Angels to co-exist with humans, sadly, so he had to kill himself sooner or later; and they might have stopped Shinji from trying to kill him later. It was his chance.

[]: Re: 2 Questions about Kaworu             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Dec 28, 2008 1:08 pm Reichu wrote: 2. If he was against killing humanity, why did he choose to die? Why didn't he try to help take down Seele?

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Dec 28, 2008 1:43 pm V wrote: it is IMPOSSIBLE for Angels to co-exist with humans, sadly, so he had to kill himself sooner or later; and they might have stopped Shinji from trying to kill him later. It was his chance.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Tue Dec 30, 2008 1:20 am I suspect that his "smile of betrayal" (to quote Reichu) toward Rei might play a part in answering these questions. He might have made a backdoor pact with Rei... (warning: fanwank ahead) By the end of episode 24, Rei has basically figured out what she is and what she can do. From there, it wasn't too hard for her to figure out what Kaworu was, and what he planned to do. She did not like the idea of humanity being annihilated, but knew that Kaworu did not know this. So she tricked Kaworu by telling him that SEELE did not want him to merge with Adam, and was sending him on a suicide mission. She promised to tell Kaworu the "real" location of Adam on the condition that she be allowed to participate in, and thereby survive, Third Impact. When Kaworu reaches Lilith and realizes that Rei betrayed him, he gets revenge with a betrayal of his own. He's going to use her affinity for humanity against her by psychologically screwing-up the person she cares most about: Shinji. This is not out of character for Rei. She did betray Gendo, after all... (/wank)

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Dec 30, 2008 2:16 am V wrote: it is IMPOSSIBLE for Angels to co-exist with humans

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Dec 30, 2008 11:56 am ...Misato said it was impossible after reading Nerv's secret files.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Dec 30, 2008 1:34 pm V wrote: ...Misato said it was impossible after reading Nerv's secret files.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Dec 30, 2008 2:30 pm Count A'ight wrote: I suspect that his "smile of betrayal" (to quote Reichu) toward Rei might play a part in answering these questions. He might have made a backdoor pact with Rei... (warning: fanwank ahead) By the end of episode 24, Rei has basically figured out what she is and what she can do. From there, it wasn't too hard for her to figure out what Kaworu was, and what he planned to do. She did not like the idea of humanity being annihilated, but knew that Kaworu did not know this. So she tricked Kaworu by telling him that SEELE did not want him to merge with Adam, and was sending him on a suicide mission. She promised to tell Kaworu the "real" location of Adam on the condition that she be allowed to participate in, and thereby survive, Third Impact. When Kaworu reaches Lilith and realizes that Rei betrayed him, he gets revenge with a betrayal of his own. He's going to use her affinity for humanity against her by psychologically screwing-up the person she cares most about: Shinji. This is not out of character for Rei. She did betray Gendo, after all... (/wank)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 30, 2008 2:38 pm Interesting fanwank Count, but as far as the audience knows, Rei and Kaworu only have one dialogue exchange in episode 24' when they meet.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Tue Dec 30, 2008 7:43 pm Only one spoken exchange. Let's not forget that they have TEH ANGEL TELEPATHY.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Tue Dec 30, 2008 9:42 pm Count A'ight wrote: Only one spoken exchange. Let's not forget that they have TEH ANGEL TELEPATHY.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Dec 31, 2008 6:59 am The Goose wrote: True, but I suppose that if they did have a exchange telepathy-wise, it wasn't very important.
